untranslatable_reason: Logistic regression probabilistic predictions and dynamically tuned probability thresholds cannot be expressed using only the allowed technical indicators and static operators. The signal requires a trained ML model (logistic regression) with time-series feature engineering (lagged returns as independent variables) and optimization logic that is beyond the scope of technical indicator-based rule expressions. The 'probability_threshold' parameter and threshold optimization for accuracy are model-level concepts, not indicator-level conditions.
translation_notes: Attempted approximation using returns as proxy for price direction signal, but this fundamentally misses the ML model specification. A proper implementation would require integration with a logistic regression model external to the rule engine, where the model generates probability predictions that are then compared against the tunable threshold parameter (0.50 with tolerance of 0.48-0.50).
